attributes.description: Alerts when the InnoDB buffer pool dirty ratio exceeds 75%. InnoDB will flush aggressively above this threshold.
attributes.name: [MySQL OTel] High buffer pool dirty page ratio

investigation_guide.blob:
## MySQL high buffer pool dirty page ratio

### What fired
Dirty pages / (dirty + clean) > 0.75 on an instance during the window.

### Why it matters
Dirty pages are modified pages awaiting flush. Above 75%, InnoDB enters aggressive async flush mode, which tanks write latency and saturates disk.

### Triage
1. Check disk write IOPS.
2. Inspect InnoDB flush and log settings.

### Remediation
- Upgrade disk.
- Tune `innodb_io_capacity` to match hardware.

### Tuning
- 75% threshold matches InnoDB's documented aggressive-flush band.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-mysqlreceiver.otel-*
// Buffer pool usage (bytes) by status: clean vs dirty; gauges
| WHERE metrics.mysql.buffer_pool.usage IS NOT NULL
    AND attributes.status IN ("dirty", "clean")
| STATS
    dirty = MAX(metrics.mysql.buffer_pool.usage) WHERE attributes.status == "dirty",
    clean = MAX(metrics.mysql.buffer_pool.usage) WHERE attributes.status == "clean"
  BY resource.attributes.mysql.instance.endpoint
| EVAL total = dirty + clean, ratio = CASE(total > 0, dirty / total, 0)
// Alert when dirty ratio > 75%; InnoDB will flush aggressively above this
| WHERE ratio > 0.75 AND total > 0
| SORT ratio DESC
